surface.name: Serverless Quickstart, Fireworks Python SDK route
surface.selection_basis: The official Serverless Quickstart says Serverless is the fastest way to start with open models and demonstrates a first API call that prints a Spanish greeting.

alternatives_considered.name: OpenAI-compatible Python
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer tab using a third-party SDK, while the Fireworks SDK is the first tab and is vendor-owned.

alternatives_considered.name: Anthropic-compatible Python
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer compatibility route using the Anthropic SDK.

alternatives_considered.name: JavaScript or cURL
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: These are peer language and transport tabs rather than separately recommended surfaces.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Fireworks developer account, obtain and fund an API key, then run the official Python Serverless first call.
assumed_prior_state: No Fireworks account, payment method, credits, API key, SDK, or request is assumed.
The developer has an email address or a Google, GitHub, or LinkedIn identity, a valid payment method and billing address, and Python with pip.
No signup, legal acceptance, billing action, key, installation, or API request was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Make your first Serverless API call
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The intended terminal is the Fireworks Python SDK printing the published DeepSeek V3.1 Spanish-greeting response, but the public official path cannot be reconstructed through signup and dashboard access.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Not observable from a complete public route because the Create Account page exposes signup choices and legal acceptance but not the provider or email verification sequence, account-creation result, or dashboard transition.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The Quickstart names and demonstrates the intended `¡Hola!` terminal, but no executable public path reaches it through account creation.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Quickstart demonstrates the desired first Serverless call, but evidence gate 8 requires the record to stop at the public signup boundary rather than infer account creation or dashboard access.

prerequisites.requirement: An email address or Google, GitHub, or LinkedIn identity for one of the current signup routes.
Signup signifies agreement to Fireworks Terms of Service and Data Processing Agreement.
A valid payment method and billing address, followed by prepaid credits before usage according to the current credit-system guide.
A Fireworks API key stored safely and exported locally.
Python and pip; the Fireworks Python SDK is alpha and requires the `--pre` install flag.

primary_path.action: Open the Serverless Quickstart and follow its Fireworks dashboard key link or account entry.
primary_path.output: The Fireworks account or dashboard route opens.
primary_path.success_signal: A new user can reach Create Account.

primary_path.action: Open Create Account and choose email, Google, GitHub, or LinkedIn signup.
details: The signup screen says that signing up agrees to the Terms of Service and Data Processing Agreement.
primary_path.input: One of the four displayed identity routes
primary_path.output: The selected email or identity-provider continuation begins.
primary_path.success_signal: No public official page documents the intermediate verification screens, the account-creation result, or the transition into the authenticated dashboard.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The inspected official pages do not provide a public recovery path for an incomplete first signup.

candidate_path_gap.reason: The public Create Account page lists email, Google, GitHub, and LinkedIn but current official pages do not enumerate any route's verification sequence, account-creation result, or transition into the authenticated dashboard.
candidate_path_gap.impact: No complete atomic candidate can connect documentation arrival to account provisioning, API key creation, billing, SDK setup, and the published Serverless call without inventing required transitions.

branches.condition: Email, Google, GitHub, or LinkedIn signup
branches.path: Begin the selected identity route; its intermediate verification and convergence are not documented publicly.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The identity route changes, but no route can be reconstructed through the required account and dashboard transition.

friction_gates.description: The signup screen offers four peer identity routes without selecting a default.
Signup states that the user agrees to the Terms of Service and Data Processing Agreement.
The post-selection verification, account result, and authenticated dashboard transition are not documented publicly.

time_to_first_success.value: The Serverless Quickstart says it makes the first call in minutes; the blocked public account route prevents end-to-end verification of that claim.

sources.title: Serverless Quickstart
sections_used: Step 1: Create and export an API key
Step 2: Make your first Serverless API call

sources.title: Sign Up
evidence_supported: Email Google GitHub and LinkedIn routes

sources.title: Onboarding
sections_used: How to get your API key
evidence_supported: Profile to User Settings to API Keys

sources.title: Concepts
evidence_supported: developer account ID generated from signup email

sources.title: How do credits work?
sections_used: What happens when I finish my $1 credit?

sources.title: Account setup & management
sections_used: Signing in from different Fireworks accounts

sources.title: Terms of Service (7.10.26)
evidence_supported: Legal acceptance applies to Service use

uncertainties.question: What is the complete verification and dashboard transition for any Fireworks signup route?
uncertainties.impact: Required account actions and the transition to API key creation cannot be recorded, so the record is blocked.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The public Create Account page lists four methods, but the current official pages do not enumerate the post-selection sequence or account-creation terminal for any of them.

uncertainties.question: Does every new account receive the $1 credit referenced by the current billing FAQ?
uncertainties.impact: If yes, payment can occur after first success; if no, a payment method and credit purchase are required beforehand.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: One official page refers to finishing `my $1 credit`, while the canonical credit-system page says to purchase credits before usage and neither states universal new-account eligibility.

uncertainties.question: What minimum credit amount can be purchased in the self-serve Billing dashboard?
uncertainties.impact: The required first cash outlay cannot be quantified.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official billing pages require prepaid credits but do not publish a minimum purchase amount.
